hub_subtitle: Business English para Gestão de Riscos e Finanças

lesson.menu_title: Writing Professional Emails I — Replying to a Partner Bank
lesson.menu_desc: Writing a formal reply to a partner bank: the words of the inbox, the connectors that link ideas, and a full reply from opening to sign-off -- 34 slides
lesson.subtitle: Lesson 9 -- Writing Professional Emails I
lesson.title_tag: Professor View -- Sergio Takashi Tanaka | Lesson 9 -- Writing Professional Emails I

listenings.text: Hi Sergio, it is Marina. A quick note before you send that reply to the partner bank. I have read your draft, and it looks formal and concise -- well done. Two small things: please attach the updated limit table, because Claire asked for it, and confirm the September figure in the very first line. In addition, remember to acknowledge her deadline; the committee meets on Friday, so a prompt reply really matters. One more point -- copy me on the email before it goes out, and I will do a final check. Thanks, talk soon.

Hi Sergio, it is Paulo from the credit desk. About the email to the partner bank -- I have just sent you the September figures, so you can confirm the exposure with confidence. I have also attached the limit table to my message; please forward it to Claire. One thing to flag: my first draft was too casual, so I made it more formal -- feel free to use my wording. Could you acknowledge Claire's inquiry today? A prompt reply will keep the committee on track for Friday. Thanks, and copy me when you reply.

extra_audio.text: First, read the inquiry from the partner bank carefully. Then open your reply with the topic. Next, confirm the main figure and attach the file they asked for. After that, add your second point with a connector such as in addition. Finally, acknowledge the deadline and close with a formal sign-off.
